text: US and Israel have discussed sending special forces into Iran to secure its stockpile of highly enriched uranium at a later stage of the war, according to Axios sources familiar with the discussions.
verificationNote: Axios citing sources familiar with discussions

text: French President Macron declared after G7 video call that 'No one can tell what US President Trump wants from this ongoing war with Iran.' Israeli officials provided conflicting war objectives to G7 leaders.
verificationNote: Macron statement after G7 call confirmed by Elysee

text: Day 18 daily report: IDF moved 36th Division deeper into southern Lebanon while cluster munitions hit central Israel. 37 Hezbollah attack waves identified on March 16. IDF expanding buffer zone toward Tyre.
verificationNote: Alma Center daily situation report

text: Iran-linked cyber proxies have become highly active since the war began. MOIS-linked 'Stryker' cyberattack demonstrates Iran retains high-end offensive cyber capability despite kinetic degradation of military targets.
verificationNote: Soufan Center IntelBrief assessment

text: Updated Iran civilian casualty count to 1,351 civilians killed including 207 children, plus 1,126 military personnel and 622 unclassified deaths. Most detailed independent casualty tracking available.
verificationNote: HRANA verified casualty database

text: Joe Kent resignation is huge — first senior Trump official to quit over the war. His claim that Iran posed 'no imminent threat' directly contradicts the Article II legal justification. Expect Congressional subpoena.
verificationNote: Analysis of confirmed resignation

text: Kuwait-flagged tanker Gas Al Ahmadiah struck near Fujairah — 23rd vessel damaged since blockade began. Iraq's oil minister in direct talks with Iran to negotiate passage for Iraqi tankers through the strait.
verificationNote: Satellite tracking and Iraqi government statement

text: USS Gerald R. Ford heading to Crete for repairs after 30-hour onboard fire. ~200 sailors treated for smoke inhalation. Carrier temporarily out of theater — significant gap in US naval power projection in the Eastern Mediterranean.
verificationNote: USNI News confirmed via Navy sources

text: Russia expanding intelligence sharing with Iran per WSJ — satellite imagery of US force locations plus improved Shahed drone components. Iran's targeting accuracy against US assets has noticeably improved since Ukraine-era drone tech transfer.
verificationNote: Based on WSJ report citing European intelligence officials

text: Trump responds to Joe Kent resignation: says Kent was 'weak on security' and a 'known leaker' who had been cut out of POTUS intelligence briefings months ago. Senior admin official tells Fox that Kent had no involvement in Iran planning.
verificationNote: Trump's response confirmed by Fox News and Townhall reporting

text: Chaharshanbe Suri unfolding in Iran tonight under near-total internet blackout. Tehran streets largely quiet but fires and fireworks reported from apartment windows. Government sent mass text warnings threatening imprisonment and flogging for 'unconventional behaviour.'
verificationNote: AFP reporting from Iran, IranWire and Iran International coverage. Internet blackout limits verification.

text: Trump says he is 'not afraid' of boots on the ground in Iran when asked if it could become another Vietnam. First direct signal he is open to ground invasion. Pentagon reportedly preparing options for Hormuz ground operation.
verificationNote: Iran International and New Republic confirmed Trump remarks with Irish PM

text: Tanker hit by projectile near UAE — first attack on Hormuz shipping in 5 days. At least 17 vessels struck since war began. Brent crude settled at $103.42/barrel, up 3.2% and now 42% higher since conflict started.
verificationNote: Alma Research Center daily report and oil market data confirmed

text: Senate Democrats led by Booker, Murphy, Kaine threatening to force repeated war powers votes and slow Senate business this week. Filed 5 resolutions demanding troop withdrawal. Republicans still blocking public hearings on the war.
verificationNote: PBS, The Hill, and Fortune confirmed Democratic strategy and filed resolutions

text: AI-generated war misinformation reaching unprecedented levels — state actors flooding social media with fake videos of explosions, destroyed cities, and downed carriers. ABC News identifies both Iranian and Russian influence operations behind coordinated disinfo campaigns.
verificationNote: ABC News, Rolling Stone, and Poynter fact-check reporting confirmed state-backed disinfo campaigns

text: Israel kills Iran intelligence minister Esmail Khatib in overnight Tehran airstrike — third senior official eliminated in 48 hours. Defense Minister Katz announces all Iranian officials now authorized targets.
verificationNote: NPR, Axios confirmed

text: Iran fires cluster munitions at central Israel for first time — Khorramshahr-4 and Qadr missiles hit Ramat Gan killing elderly couple. IRGC explicitly frames as revenge for Larijani assassination.
verificationNote: Haaretz, Times of Israel confirmed

text: South Pars gas field struck by Israeli jets — two refineries halted (100M cubic meters/day). First upstream energy strike of the war. Brent spikes. Iran threatens retaliation against Saudi, UAE, Qatar energy sites.
verificationNote: Bloomberg, CTV confirmed

text: Turkey FM Fidan heading to Riyadh for multilateral meeting. 10+ countries attending including Pakistan, Egypt, UAE, Qatar. Aim: diplomatic pathway to end Iran war.
verificationNote: Daily Sabah confirmed

text: Chaharshanbe Suri aftermath: footage shows bonfires across Tehran despite regime crackdown threats. PMOI claims 15 resistance operations. Reza Pahlavi says ready to return. Internet still blacked out.
verificationNote: Internet blackout limits verification

text: Mojtaba Khamenei rejects ceasefire proposals from two intermediary countries. Says 'not the right time for peace.' Still no public appearance since appointment — health status unknown.
verificationNote: India TV News confirmed

text: US CENTCOM deploys 5,000-lb bunker busters against hardened Iranian antiship missile sites along Hormuz. Analysts call it opening salvo of multi-week campaign to reopen strait.
verificationNote: NBC News confirmed
